papers.title_en: A Time-Domain Method of Auxiliary Sources for Analyzing Transient Electromagnetic Interactions with GSTC-Modeled Metasurfacess
papers.authors: Minas Kouroublakis, Nikolaos L. Tsitsas, Yehuda Leviatan
papers.evidence: 瞬态电磁响应的时域建模与阻抗变换
papers.tldr: 本研究提出了一种结合时域辅助源法（TD-MAS）与广义片层跃迁条件（GSTC）的新型公式，用于分析二维超表面的瞬态电磁响应。该方法通过将频域阻抗型GSTC转换为因果且基于卷积的时域表示，并将其集成到MAS框架中，实现了对超表面电磁相互作用的精确建模，为瞬态电磁分析提供了高效的数值方案。
papers.abstract_en: This paper presents a time domain (TD) formulation for modeling the transient electromagnetic response of two-dimensional (2D) metasurfaces using the Method of Auxiliary Sources (MAS) combined with the Generalized Sheet Transition Condition (GSTC). In the proposed approach, the frequency-domain impedance type GSTC is transformed into a causal, convolution-based TD representation and integrated within the MAS formulation. rfaces.

papers.title_en: PointNeRT: A Physics Aware Neural Ray Tracing Surrogate for Propagation Channel Modeling
papers.authors: Zhuoyin Li, Ruisi He, Mi Yang, Ziyi Qi, Zhengyu Zhang, Jiahui Han, Haoxiang Zhang, Bingcheng Liu
papers.evidence: 用于传播信道建模的神经代理模型
papers.tldr: 针对传统射线追踪依赖精确网格和材料属性导致在复杂场景中扩展性差的问题，本文提出PointNeRT，一种物理感知的神经射线追踪代理模型。该模型直接以点云为输入，采用受物理约束引导的逐跳建模策略，无需显式构建网格或手动定义电磁规则即可预测多径传播和功率衰减。实验证明其在移动场景中具有良好的泛化能力，实现了物理引导的信道建模。
papers.abstract_en: Ray tracing (RT) has emerged as a key tool for propagation channel modeling and network planning. Conventional RT is based on electromagnetic (EM) wave theory and its application relies on detailed mesh-based environment representations and material properties. In realistic environments, limited environmental geometry and material uncertainties hinder its scalability to complex scenarios. In this paper, we propose a novel physics aware neural RT surrogate named PointNeRT to address these limitations. The proposed model directly takes point clouds as environmental input, and efficiently reconstruct multipath without explicitly constructing mesh models or manually defining EM interaction rules. PointNeRT adopts a hop-by-hop modeling strategy guided by physical interaction constraints. It supports sequential prediction of multipath propagation and power attenuation. Numerical results and experiments demonstrate that the proposed method implicitly captures surface normal characteristics and EM material effects. It further achieves robust generalization in mobility scenarios and provides a physics-guided neural modeling of multipath propagation.

papers.title_en: LLM-Driven Design Space Exploration of FPGA-based Accelerators
papers.authors: Vinamra Sharma, Xingjian Fu, Jude Haris, José Cano
papers.evidence: 大语言模型驱动的硬件加速器设计空间探索
papers.tldr: 针对现代AI工作负载下FPGA加速器设计空间复杂、手动探索耗时耗力的问题，本文提出了SECDA-DSE框架。该框架将大语言模型（LLM）集成到SECDA协同设计生态中，通过结合检索增强生成（RAG）和思维链（CoT）提示词技术，实现了自动化的硬件配置生成与推理引导探索。研究还引入了反馈循环以进行强化微调，显著降低了对领域专家的依赖，并在Zynq-7000 FPGA上验证了生成设计的可行性。
papers.abstract_en: Designing field-programmable gate array (FPGA)-based accelerators for modern artificial intelligence workloads requires navigating a large and complex hardware design space encompassing architectural parameters, dataflow strategies, and memory hierarchies, making the process time-consuming and resource-intensive. While the SECDA methodology enables rapid hardware-software co-design of accelerators through SystemC simulation and FPGA execution, identifying optimal accelerator configurations still requires substantial manual effort and domain expertise. This work presents SECDA-DSE, a framework that integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) into the SECDA ecosystem, comprising tools built around SECDA to automate the design space exploration (DSE) of FPGA-based accelerators. SECDA-DSE combines a structured DSE Explorer for generating accelerator configurations with an LLM Stack that performs reasoning-guided exploration using retrieval-augmented generation and chain-of-thought prompting, alongside a feedback loop that enables reinforced fine-tuning for continuous improvement. We demonstrate the feasibility of SECDA-DSE through an initial high-level synthesis based evaluation of a generated accelerator design that meets synthesis timing and resource constraints on an Zynq-7000 FPGA.

papers.title_en: Bridging the Last Mile of Circuit Design: PostEDA-Bench, a Hierarchical Benchmark for PPA Convergence and DRC Fixing
papers.authors: Pengju Liu, Nuo Xu, Jinwei Tang, Yu Cao, Caiwen Ding
papers.evidence: 基于大语言模型的代理用于电路设计中的PPA收敛和DRC修复
papers.tldr: 本研究针对电子设计自动化（EDA）中DRC修复和PPA收敛的“最后一公里”问题，提出了PostEDA-Bench基准测试。该基准包含145个分层任务，涵盖DRC基础与推理、单目标与多目标PPA优化，并支持多种EDA工具链。通过对8种主流大模型的评估，揭示了当前模型在复杂推理和多目标权衡方面的局限性，为提升LLM在电路设计中的应用提供了重要参考。
papers.abstract_en: LLM-based agents are increasingly applied to the "last mile" of Electronic Design Automation (EDA): repairing residual sign-off Design Rule Check (DRC) violations and converging Power-Performance-Area (PPA) targets after tool runs. Existing EDA-LLM benchmarks, however, omit DRC fixing entirely and rely on flat hierarchies tied to a single toolchain. We introduce PostEDA-Bench, a hierarchical benchmark with 145 tasks across DRC-Essential, DRC-Reasoning, PPA-Mono, and PPA-Multi, supported by EDA toolchains with machine-checkable evaluation. Across eight commercial and open-source LLMs under multiple agent scaffolds, we find that agents handle synthetic DRC-Essential and single-objective PPA-Mono reasonably well but degrade sharply on the more practical DRC-Reasoning, where the best success rate is 36.66%, and PPA-Multi, where the best success rate is 20.00%; vision augmentation consistently enhances DRC-Bench; and trade-off reasoning, rather than knob knowledge, is the dominant PPA-Multi bottleneck.
